title: US embassy in Riyadh targeted by drones; Saudi Arabia issues shelter-in-place order for 3 cities
content: Two drones struck the US embassy in Riyadh early Tuesday, causing a small fire and minor damage. Saudi authorities confirmed the attack, with air defense systems intercepting four drones aimed at the diplomatic quarter. The US embassy issued a shelter-in-place advisory for American citizens in the region as a precautionary measure.

title: Bengaluru actor gags live-in partner with lover’s help, stabs him to death
content: An actress, her boyfriend, and his associate were arrested for the murder of her live-in partner in Bengaluru. The trio allegedly stabbed 29-year-old Mohan Krishna after an alcohol party, following disagreements over the actress's intention to marry her boyfriend. The accused lured the victim to a desolate spot for the fatal attack.

title: 'India would've been knocked out': Ex-Pakistan star sparks controversy after India's semifinal entry
content: Mohammad Amir faced criticism after his prediction that India wouldn't reach the T20 World Cup 2026 semi-finals proved incorrect. Despite India's win, Amir highlighted a controversial dismissal of Shimron Hetmyer, believing it could have altered the match's outcome. He also questioned India's overall performance, citing fielding errors and reliance on Jasprit Bumrah.

title: After signing Pentagon deal, Sam Altman to Silicon Valley: You have double standards
content: OpenAI has secured a contract with the Department of War for classified network deployment, following earlier discussions about non-classified work. CEO Sam Altman emphasized the need for AI companies to support the US government's mission, pushing back against perceived hypocrisy in the tech industry's stance on geopolitical competition with China.

title: 'You’ll soon find out response': Trump reacts to drone attack on US embassy in Riyadh
content: US President Donald Trump has vowed swift retaliation following a drone strike on the American embassy in Riyadh. The Saudi defense ministry confirmed two drones hit the compound, causing minor damage and a small fire. This attack occurs amidst escalating tensions and Iranian strikes targeting Gulf states with US bases.

title: CEO of multi-billion software company Workday says AI Agents are not replacing us
content: Workday CEO Aneel Bhusri reassured investors that AI won't replace essential HR software, despite a recent stock dip following a revenue forecast.  The company, which announced 2% workforce cuts, is developing AI agents to enhance, not replace, its enterprise systems, aiming for a consumption-based model.

title: Every India-England semi-final winner has won the T20 World Cup — trend to continue?
content: India and England renew their T20 World Cup semi-final rivalry at Wankhede, marking their third consecutive clash in the last four. Both previous encounters saw the winner go on to lift the trophy, adding significant intrigue to this high-stakes encounter. The teams hold a balanced record in World Cup semi-finals, with one win each.

title: Happy Holi 2026: Best messages, quotes, wishes and images to share
content: Holi 2026, celebrated on Wednesday, March 4th, marks the arrival of spring with vibrant colors and joyous festivities. The festival commemorates the mythological triumph of good over evil, symbolized by Holika Dahan bonfires and the devotion of Prahlad. It's a time for reconnecting, forgiving, and celebrating life's colorful moments with loved ones.

title: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan: I was shocked to find China's Huawei has hired engineers who can...
content: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan expressed shock at Huawei's 100+ CPU architects, developing alternatives to advanced chip tools and lithography. He warned China is rapidly closing the semiconductor gap through engineering and infrastructure optimization, even excelling in open-source AI. Tan urged the US against complacency, stating China could soon lead.

title: How 'soul searching' helped Sanju Samson score match-winning 97
content: Sanju Samson's spectacular 97 not out powered India into the T20 World Cup 2026 semifinals. He credits time away from social media and self-reflection for his return to form. Samson's innings secured a five-wicket victory against West Indies. This performance marks a significant comeback for the batsman after a challenging period.

title: Lunar Eclipse Visibility: Blood Moon to appear over India; best seen after 6:26 PM
content: Skywatchers anticipate a rare Blood Moon lunar eclipse on March 3, 2026, visible across India after moonrise. This celestial event, occurring in Leo, may bring personal and professional changes. While visible in many countries, India will only witness the latter stages due to the eclipse coinciding with moonrise.

title: Friends kidnap Delhi bizman for gold, kill him after extortion bid fails
content: A 48-year-old Delhi businessman, Anroop Gupta, missing since February 18, was found murdered and his body dismembered in a Mathura canal. Police arrested four individuals, including a woman, who allegedly abducted and killed Gupta for money after he refused to pay them and threatened to report them.

title: Who is Jason Anderson’s wife? Kenzie Meads embroiled in domestic violence report
content: Supercross rider Jason Anderson is under scrutiny after reports claimed he was arrested in December 2025 on a domestic violence charge involving his wife, Kenzie Meads. Deputies reportedly responded to a 911 call at their Orange County home. The case is still pending, and Anderson has not spoken publicly. Fans have shared concern online, especially after both deactivated Instagram.

title: 'She has down drome': Brittany Mahomes faces brutal fan reactions following NFL WAGs lineup video
content: Brittany Mahomes drew intense online reactions after appearing in the Sports Illustrated Swimsuit NFL WAGs promo video. While some fans praised her introduction and confidence, many mocked her inclusion and appearance, sparking a heated social media debate. The mixed response highlights the scrutiny NFL partners face and how public visibility often invites both admiration and harsh criticism.

title: Wild's offer for Vincent Trocheck pressures Rangers before trade deadline
content: According to the Minnesota Wild, a standing trade deal is on Vincent Trocheck, which is putting pressure on the New York Rangers before the conclusion of the NHL trade deadline. As there are playoff implications and salary cap implications in play, both teams have some decisions to make that could define their future perspectives of the playoffs, as well as how they would build around their rosters in the long term.

title: NFL Trade News: Jets eye Broncos' $12M QB after Fields plan fails
content: The New York Jets are exploring veteran options for their quarterback position. They might sign two veteran players to support their primary signal caller Justin Fields. General Manager Darren Mougey is reportedly a fan of Denver Broncos quarterback Jarrett Stidham. The team is evaluating all possible scenarios for the 2026 season. Stidham is under contract with Denver but could be a trade option as he is on his final year.

title: Los Angeles Clippers vs Golden State Warriors injury report: Who's playing, injured and record
content: The Los Angeles Clippers face the Golden State Warriors on March 2nd, with both teams vying for a play-in spot. Injuries heavily impact this matchup; the Warriors are without Stephen Curry, while the Clippers have questionable players. Availability will be crucial as the Clippers aim to improve their standing against a Warriors team struggling offensively.

title: 'Singapore' to ‘Goat’s Nest’: Polling station names from 2002 rolls leave Telangana voters baffled
content: Bizarre polling station names like 'Singapore', 'For Pakistan', and 'Slingshot' are causing confusion in Telangana's 2002 electoral rolls. Residents struggle to locate their names on the list, which is being used for mapping, due to these nonsensical entries. Officials admit the errors and promise to investigate, while the Election Commission states data was hosted as received.

title: Houston Rockets vs Washington Wizards injury report: Who's playing, injured and record
content: Houston Rockets, bolstered by a strong record and recent wins against Washington, visit the Wizards on March 2, 2026. Despite significant injuries impacting both teams, Houston's healthier rotation and defensive prowess give them a clear advantage. Washington faces a tougher challenge with key players sidelined, forcing younger talent into prominent roles.

title: Russia remains largest supplier of crude to India in February, Saudi Arabia close second
content: Saudi Arabia significantly boosted crude oil supplies to India in February, nearly matching Russia's top position. This surge, the highest in six years, reflects a shift by refiners away from some Russian volumes. However, ongoing Gulf disruptions may force India to seek alternative import routes, potentially altering the supply landscape.

title: Yash’s Toxic has made almost Rs 600 crore from pre-business alone, says producer
content: Yash’s upcoming film Toxic: A Fairy Tale for Grown-Ups hasn’t hit theatres yet, but if veteran producer G Dhananjayan is to be believed, the film has already clocked staggering numbers. Toxic is set to release on March 19 alongside Dhurandhar 2, a sequel to an established franchise. But Dhananjayan doesn’t see that as a risky move.

title: Watch: House panel releases video of Bill and Hillary Clinton Epstein depositions
content: House Republicans have released deposition videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton regarding Jeffrey Epstein. Both former leaders denied knowledge of his crimes, with Bill Clinton recalling a property dispute conversation with Donald Trump. Hillary Clinton suggested questioning Trump directly. Epstein died in jail in 2019 awaiting trial.

title: How 15 balls reignited India's T20 World Cup campaign before Sanju's 97
content: While Sanju's 97 in Kolkata will be remembered, his short-lived but significant innings against Zimbabwe three days prior had brought momentum to India's T20 World Cup campaign.

title: Trump says Op Epic Fury was US' 'last best chance' to eliminate 'intolerable' threats
content: President Trump stated that operations against Iran are expected to last four to five weeks, with the US prepared to extend the campaign if necessary. The stated objectives include destroying Iran's missile and naval capabilities, preventing nuclear weapon acquisition, and halting support for terrorist groups, framing the action as a response to decades of attacks.

title: Air India Express to resume Muscat flights from March 3; Gulf routes remain suspended
content: Air India Express will resume flights to and from Muscat on Tuesday, March 3, after a temporary halt due to the Middle East conflict. Services to Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE will continue to be suspended until March 3. The airline cited airspace closures in the region impacting operations.

title: Who could replace Iran if it withdraws from FIFA World Cup in US?
content: Iran's participation in the upcoming FIFA World Cup in the United States is uncertain due to escalating Middle East conflict. Coordinated attacks have led to retaliatory missile firings, prompting concerns about the team's ability to attend or be allowed entry. FIFA's stance remains to monitor developments as the situation unfolds.

title: How much do China, India, Japan depend on Middle Eastern crude, gas?
content: How much are China, Japan, India, and South Korea exposed to Middle East oil supply disruption risks? Where do their strategic oil reserves stand? Let’s take a look:

title: NHL trade update: Seattle Kraken sign defenseman Olofsson to a one-year deal ahead of deadline
content: The Seattle Kraken have signed defenseman Gustav Olofsson to a one-year contract. This move adds experienced depth to their blue line as the team competes for a playoff spot. Olofsson previously played for Seattle's AHL affiliate, the Coachella Valley Firebirds. He brings NHL experience from previous stints with the Minnesota Wild and Montreal Canadiens.

title: Bigg Boss 16 fame Manya Singh stranded in Dubai amid Middle East tensions
content: Reality star Manya Singh, known from Bigg Boss 16, finds herself in a precarious situation in Dubai as her return flight gets grounded amid rising tensions in the Middle East. From her 65th-floor hotel room, she reported hearing unsettling blast-like noises, heightening her sense of fear.

title: Modi govt's silence on Khamenei assassination not neutral but abdication: Sonia
content: When the targeted killing of a foreign leader draws no clear defence of sovereignty or international law from our country and impartiality is abandoned, it raises serious doubts about the direction and credibility of our foreign policy, says Sonia Gandhi said in her article.

content: Petitioner-NGO says right to safe blood transfusion is a fundamental part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution; it says NAT is a safer testing mechanism to ensure the supply of safe and infection-free blood to recipients

title: Will petrol, diesel rates be hiked in India as crude nears $80 mark on Middle East tensions?
content: Pump prices for petrol and diesel have remained unchanged since April 2022. Domestic fuel prices have stayed steady even when global fuel rates climbed due to higher crude costs.

title: Apple iPhone 17e with A19 chip launched in India for Rs 64,999: All you need to know
content: Apple unveils the iPhone 17e, its most affordable model, starting at Rs 64,900 in India. This budget-friendly device boasts MagSafe wireless charging, the powerful A19 chip, and double the base storage.  With a refined design and enhanced camera capabilities, it offers significant upgrades for consumers seeking value.

title: Anubhav Sinha reacts to Deepika Padukone's 8-hour shift row
content: Amid the ongoing debate around actors demanding fixed 8–9 hour shifts, filmmaker Anubhav Sinha weighed in during a recent chat, offering a measured and practical take on the controversy. When asked whether he would be okay signing an actor who insists on working only 8 or 9 hours a day, Sinha said the answer is simple.

title: Nayab Saini Wears Saffron Turban, Congress Says He Has Eyes On Punjab Polls
content: Haryana Chief Minister Nayab Singh Saini presented the state budget on Monday, but it wasn't just the financial announcements that grabbed attention.

title: No Answers, Then All At Once: Digital Cheating Scandal Hits Madhya Pradesh
content: What unfolded inside an examination lab in Ratlam during the Excise Constable Recruitment Examination 2024 is now being described as one of the most serious digital irregularity cases in Madhya Pradesh since the Vyapam scandal.

title: UAE, Qatar Urge Allies To Press Trump For Quick De-Escalation In Iran
content: The United Arab Emirates and Qatar are privately lobbying allies to help them persuade President Donald Trump to reach for an off-ramp that would keep US military operations against Iran short, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Indian National Killed In Oman As Oil Tanker Hit Off Muscat Coast
content: An Indian national was killed on Monday when an explosive-laden drone boat struck an oil tanker off the coast of Muscat Governorate, according to officials.

title: 116 Flights Cancelled At Mumbai Airport Amid Gulf Airspace Closure
content: The ongoing military escalation in the Middle East, involving the United States, Israel and Iran, has triggered severe disruptions to global aviation, leaving hundreds of flights cancelled and passengers stranded.

title: Lebanon Imposes "Immediate Ban" On Hezbollah Military Activities
content: Lebanon's government has decided on an "immediate ban" of Iran-backed Hezbollah's military and security activity, Prime Minister Nawaf Salam said Monday, in an unprecedented move as Israel retaliated to rocket fire.

title: From Aircraft To Lives: Why "Friendly Fire" Can Be Such A Big Enemy
content: For military aircraft flying in contested airspace monitored by air defence systems operating in their highest alert level, the risk of a blue-on-blue or friendly fire incident rises exponentially.

title: Exclusive: Will Iran Get To Pick Its Leader After Khamenei? What Israeli Minister Gideon Sa'ar Said
content: Neither the US nor Israel will decide who will lead Iran after Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's assassination, an Israeli minister has told NDTV, stressing that it's for the Iranian people to choose their next leader.

title: West Asia conflict: Govt may ask companies to cut exports, increase auto fuel, LPG supplies
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: In SC, temple board to defend women's entry ban in Sabarimala temple
